titleStmt.title: Letter from Herbert Wood to Sir Robert Chalmers, 29 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Herbert Wood

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Herbert Wood (1861 – 1955), assistant deputy keeper with the Public Record Office, to the Under-Secretary Sir Robert Chalmers (1858 – 1938). This letter was sent by Wood to forward on the Forty-Eighth Report of the Deputy Keeper of the Public Records and Keeper of the State Papers in Ireland in accordance with the twenty-fourth section of the Public Records (Ireland) Act.Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the Easter Rising in 1916.

correspAction.placeName: Public Record Office of Ireland, Four Courts, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
PUBLIC RECORD OFFICE OF IRELAND 29 th July 1916 Sir I beg to forward herewith for submission
to Their Excellencies the Lords Justices pursuant
to the twentyâfourth section of the Public
Records (Ireland) Act and with the sanction
of the Rt Hon the Master of the Rolls, the
FortyâEighth Report of the Deputy Keeper of
the Public Records and Keeper of the State
Papers in Ireland and shall be obliged
for an early acknowledgement of the
receipt I have the honour to be
Sir
Your obedient servant Herbert Wood Asst t Deputy Keeper of the Records The Rt Hon The Under Secretary Dublin Castle A.N.S.
I think this Report is
rightly addressed to the Lords
Justices as they were in
office on the
26 th July when the Report was signed by the Deputy Keeper
